quality_note: Cumulative totals are strong, but combatant/civilian separation requires explicit modeling.

quality_note: Core direct-death estimates were nulled after the mortality audit because the ceasefire-period reporting windows mix newly killed people with bodies newly retrieved or with casualties reported after attacks that mostly occurred before the ceasefire.
formula_note: No clean within-episode direct-death numerator could be defended after the audit. The source windows mix new killings with bodies retrieved from rubble and, near the start boundary, with casualties reported after attacks that mostly occurred before the ceasefire.
uncertainty_note: Core direct-death estimates were nulled after the mortality audit because the ceasefire-period reporting windows mix newly killed people with bodies newly retrieved or with casualties reported after attacks that mostly occurred before the ceasefire.

quality_note: Combatant deaths after January 2025 remain weakly sourced. The October 2025 endpoint also contains retroactively approved fatalities, so conflict-level direct-death use is stronger than precise within-phase timing assignment.
formula_note: For deaths: low uses clear-civilian or directly observed floor; best allocates 50% of residual after subtracting civ_low and mil_best; high assigns all non-military residual deaths to civilians.
uncertainty_note: Boundary and totals are good, but Palestinian militant deaths after January 2025 are modeled rather than directly sourced.

quality_note: Useful bounded disproportionality proxy, but not a full child-vs-adult-male civilian risk estimate.
formula_note: (children deaths share of all recorded direct deaths) / (children share of Gaza population) = (10,600 / 24,620) / 0.47.
uncertainty_note: Proxy only. Numerator is the public all-deaths child count near the boundary, not a civilian-only child series.

quality_note: Publishes a transparent female/women disproportionality signal without forcing unsafe adult-male residual math.
formula_note: (women deaths share of all recorded direct deaths) / (female share of Gaza population) = (7,200 / 24,620) / 0.493.
uncertainty_note: Conservative proxy. The source-native women category is likely closer to adult women than to all females, while the denominator is all females because an adult-women baseline is not used in this fallback case.
